title: Software Image Inventory and Version Summary
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`. via custom scripted input polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/compliance/detail?complianceType=IMAGE` (deploy per docs/guides/catalyst-center.md § Custom Scripted Inputs). Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `imageCompliance` (string), `runningVersion` (string), `targetVersion` (string), `deviceFamily` (string), `deviceName` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `upgradeStatus` (string). CIM data model: Change.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + custom scripted input for SWIM API
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" | stats dc(deviceName) as device_count values(runningVersion) as versions by deviceFamily, platformId | sort -device_count
description: Provides a complete inventory of software images running across all managed devices, grouped by platform family and running version, using Catalyst Center's SWIM data.
value: Knowing exactly which software versions are running on every device is foundational for firmware compliance, vulnerability management, and upgrade planning.
implementation:
The Cisco Catalyst TA does not poll SWIM data natively. Create a custom scripted input that authenticates to Catalyst Center and polls the SWIM endpoints.

API endpoints:
• `POST /dna/system/api/v1/auth/token` — authenticate and get a token
• `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-device-image-updates` — get device image update status
• `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/image/importation` — list available images
• `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/compliance/detail?complianceType=IMAGE` — image compliance

Create a scripted input in your custom app:

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_catalyst_swim/bin/collect_swim.py]
interval = 3600
sourcetype = cisco:dnac:swim
index = catalyst
disabled = 0
```

The script should:
1. Authenticate to Catalyst Center using `POST /dna/system/api/v1/auth/token`
2. Call `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-device-image-updates` to get current running versions
3. Output JSON events with fields: `deviceName`, `deviceFamily`, `platformId`, `runningVersion`, `targetVersion`, `imageCompliance`, `lastUpgradeDate`
4. Store credentials in Splunk's credential store (passwords.conf) — never hardcode

Alternatively, configure Catalyst Center Platform event notifications for SWIM events and send to Splunk via HEC webhook.
visualization: Table (platform, version, device count), Bar chart (version distribution), Single value (total managed devices).

references.title: Catalyst Center SWIM Compliance API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Lab and staging devices running many different firmware versions.** Lab equipment intentionally runs various firmware versions for testing, creating apparent version fragmentation. Distinguish by checking whether the multi-version devices are in lab or staging `siteId` values. Suppress by filtering lab sites from the version summary using a `catalyst_excluded_sites` lookup.

**Devices mid-upgrade showing transitional firmware state.** During a SWIM upgrade campaign, devices being upgraded show the old version until the upgrade completes. The version summary may show more versions than expected during the upgrade window. Distinguish by checking `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim"` for `upgradeStatus=IN_PROGRESS` devices. Suppress by annotating the version summary with the active upgrade status.

**Stack members running different firmware versions during a rolling upgrade.** In stacked switch configurations, individual stack members may run different versions during a rolling upgrade. The version summary may show both old and new versions for the same stack. Distinguish by checking whether `deviceFamily` is a stackable platform and multiple versions appear for the same `hostname` prefix. No suppression needed — track as an expected transitional state.

**Golden image target changed but devices not yet upgraded.** After changing the golden image target, all devices still running the old version appear as non-compliant. The version summary shows the old version as the most common until upgrades are complete. Distinguish by correlating with audit logs for golden image changes. Do not suppress — this is a real finding that should drive upgrade planning.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **swim** (or **device**) modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim` or `cisco:dnac:device`.
• Catalyst Center **2.3.5+** with SWIM (Software Image Management) configured — golden images defined for each device family.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE** for SWIM data access.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
The TA polls `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/image/importation` or the SWIM API at the configured interval. Each event represents one device's firmware status.

Key fields:
• `runningVersion`: the IOS-XE version currently running on the device (e.g., "17.9.4a").
• `targetVersion`: the golden image version the device should be running.
• `deviceName`: device hostname.
• `deviceFamily`: device platform family (e.g., "Switches and Hubs", "Routers", "Wireless Controller").
• `platformId`: hardware PID (e.g., "C9300-48P").
• `upgradeStatus`: "COMPLIANT", "NON_COMPLIANT", "IN_PROGRESS", "SCHEDULED".

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" OR (sourcetype="cisco:dnac:device" isnotnull(softwareVersion))
| stats dc(deviceName) as device_count values(platformId) as platforms by runningVersion, deviceFamily
| sort deviceFamily, runningVersion
| eventstats sum(device_count) as family_total by deviceFamily
| eval version_pct=round(device_count/family_total*100,1)
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" OR (sourcetype="cisco:dnac:device" ...)`**: Uses SWIM data when available, falls back to device inventory for version information.
• **`stats dc(deviceName) ... by runningVersion, deviceFamily`**: Shows the firmware version distribution per device family. Multiple versions within a family indicate upgrade progress or fragmentation.
• **`version_pct`**: Percentage of devices in each family running each version — helps identify how much of the fleet has been upgraded.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Design > Image Repository > Software Images. Compare the version distribution with the Splunk results.
• **Golden image check:** Verify that the `targetVersion` matches the golden image defined in SWIM for each device family.
• **Vendor UI parity:** cross-reference the Splunk results with the corresponding **Catalyst Center > Assurance** page for the same time window to confirm counts and values match.
Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Version distribution bar chart by device family, pie chart showing COMPLIANT vs NON_COMPLIANT percentages, and a table of non-golden versions with device counts.
• **Upgrade campaign tracking:** Use the version distribution to measure upgrade campaign progress — track the percentage of devices on the golden image over time.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Version field not populated:** Some Catalyst Center versions use `softwareVersion` instead of `runningVersion`. Run `| fieldsummary` to identify the correct field.
• **Lab devices skewing the distribution:** Filter lab sites from the analysis using a `catalyst_excluded_sites` lookup.
• **No SWIM events arriving:** SWIM requires a custom scripted input; verify the script is deployed and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages.
grandmaExplanation: We catalog the software version running on every network device so you always know what is out there and what needs updating.
